title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
description:
A singly linked list is given as a plain list of values `values`, head-to-tail, along with an integer `n`. Remove the `n`th node from the end of the list (1-indexed, so `n = 1` removes the last node) and return the resulting list of values. Do it in a single pass over the list.

**Example**

```
values = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], n = 2   ->  [1, 2, 3, 5]
```
starter_code:
def remove_nth_from_end(values, n):
    # values: list of node values head-to-tail.
    # n: remove the nth node counting from the end (1-indexed).
    # Return the resulting list of values.
    pass
hints: Use a dummy node before the head so removing the actual head (when n equals the list length) doesn't need special-casing.
Advance a fast pointer n steps ahead of a slow pointer (both starting at dummy), then move both forward together until fast reaches the last node; slow.next is now the node to remove.

solutions.name: Two pointers with n-node gap, dummy head
solutions.explanation: Build real nodes from values with a dummy node in front of the head so the head itself can be removed uniformly. Advance a fast pointer n steps from dummy. Then advance both fast and a slow pointer (starting at dummy) one step at a time until fast reaches the last node. At that point slow.next is exactly the node that is n from the end, so unlink it: slow.next = slow.next.next. Read the values back out starting from dummy.next.
solutions.code:
class _Node:
    __slots__ = ("val", "next")
    def __init__(self, val):
        self.val = val
        self.next = None


def remove_nth_from_end(values, n):
    dummy = _Node(None)
    tail = dummy
    for v in values:
        tail.next = _Node(v)
        tail = tail.next

    fast = dummy
    for _ in range(n):
        fast = fast.next

    slow = dummy
    while fast.next is not None:
        fast = fast.next
        slow = slow.next

    slow.next = slow.next.next

    out = []
    node = dummy.next
    while node is not None:
        out.append(node.val)
        node = node.next
    return out
solutions.complexity: Time O(n), Space O(n) (excluding the O(1) extra pointer space, O(n) is for building/reading the list)
